description: Nuphar advena is a species of Nuphar native throughout the eastern United States and in some parts of Canada, as well as Mexico and Cuba. It is locally naturalized in Britain.
origin: Europe, northwest Africa and western Asia.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)
care.used_for_details: (NC State ethnobotany) It has been used medicinally and as a food source

description: Nuphar lutea, the yellow water-lily, is an aquatic plant of the family Nymphaeaceae, native to northern temperate and some subtropical regions of Europe, northwest Africa, and western Asia. This species was used as a food source and in medicinal practices from prehistoric times with potential research and medical applications going forward.
origin: Europe, northwest Africa and western Asia.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)
care.toxicity_note: Human: toxic. Animal: toxic. Parts: Whole plant, highest concentrations in root. (Source: TPPT/Agroscope)
care.toxic_parts: Whole plant, highest concentrations in root
care.used_for_details: (NC State ethnobotany) It has been used medicinally and as a food source

description: Nuphar polysepala, also known as the great yellow pond-lily, wokas, or wocus, is a perennial, rhizomatous, aquatic herb in the genus Nuphar. It has floating green leaves and yellow flowers.
origin: Europe, northwest Africa and western Asia.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)
care.used_for_details: (NC State ethnobotany) It has been used medicinally and as a food source

description: Nuphar pumila, the least water-lily, is a perennial, rhizomatous, aquatic herb in the family Nymphaeaceae native to subarctic and temperate Eurasia.
origin: Europe, northwest Africa and western Asia.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)
care.toxicity_note: Human: toxic. Animal: weak toxic. Parts: Whole plant, highest concentrations in root. (Source: TPPT/Agroscope)
care.toxic_parts: Whole plant, highest concentrations in root
care.used_for_details: (NC State ethnobotany) It has been used medicinally and as a food source

description: Nuphar variegata is rhizomatous, perennial, aquatic herb in the water lily family Nymphaeaceae native to much of Canada and the northernmost of the United States.
origin: Europe, northwest Africa and western Asia.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)
care.used_for_details: (NC State ethnobotany) It has been used medicinally and as a food source

description: Nymphaea alba – commonly called the white water lily, European white water lily, or white nenuphar – is an aquatic flowering plant in the family Nymphaeaceae. It is native to North Africa, temperate Asia, Europe, and tropical Asia.
origin: Europe, Asia, Africa, the Americas, Austria

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)
care.toxicity_note: Human: toxic. Animal: toxic. Parts: Flower, rhizome (or whole plant). (Source: TPPT/Agroscope)
care.used_for_details: (NC State ethnobotany) Tannins extracted from rhizomes are used in dyeing wool a purple-black or brown
care.edible_parts: In some parts of the world the leaves and flowers are eaten in moderation.

description: Nymphaea ampla, also known as the dotleaf waterlily, is a species of flowering plant in the family Nymphaeaceae. It is native to Texas, Florida, Mexico, Central America, the Caribbean, and northern and western South America.
origin: Europe, Asia, Africa, the Americas, Austria

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)
care.used_for_details: (NC State ethnobotany) Tannins extracted from rhizomes are used in dyeing wool a purple-black or brown
care.edible_parts: In some parts of the world the leaves and flowers are eaten in moderation.

description: Nymphaea candida is a species of perennial, aquatic, rhizomatous herb in the family Nymphaeaceae native to the region spanning from Europe to Siberia and Western Himalaya.
origin: Europe, Asia, Africa, the Americas, Austria

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)
care.used_for_details: (NC State ethnobotany) Tannins extracted from rhizomes are used in dyeing wool a purple-black or brown
care.edible_parts: In some parts of the world the leaves and flowers are eaten in moderation.

description: Nymphaea lotus, the white Egyptian lotus, tiger lotus, white lotus, or Egyptian water-lily, is a flowering plant of the family Nymphaeaceae.
origin: Europe, Asia, Africa, the Americas, Austria

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)
care.used_for_details: (NC State ethnobotany) Tannins extracted from rhizomes are used in dyeing wool a purple-black or brown
care.edible_parts: In some parts of the world the leaves and flowers are eaten in moderation.

description: Nymphaea maculata is a species of waterlily native to tropical Africa.

Nymphaea nouchali, often known by its synonym Nymphaea stellata, or by common names blue lotus, star lotus, red water lily, dwarf aquarium lily, blue water lily, blue star water lily or manel flower, is a water lily of genus Nymphaea. It is native to southern and eastern parts of Asia, and is the national flower of Bangladesh and Sri Lanka.This species is usually considered to include the blue Egyptian lotus N. nouchali var. caerulea.
origin: Europe, Asia, Africa, the Americas, Austria

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)
care.used_for_details: (NC State ethnobotany) Tannins extracted from rhizomes are used in dyeing wool a purple-black or brown
care.edible_parts: In some parts of the world the leaves and flowers are eaten in moderation.

description: Nymphaea odorata, also known as the American white waterlily, fragrant water-lily, beaver root, fragrant white water lily, white water lily, sweet-scented white water lily, and sweet-scented water lily, is an aquatic plant belonging to the genus Nymphaea. It can commonly be found in shallow lakes, ponds, and permanent slow moving waters throughout North America where it ranges from Central America to northern Canada. It is also reported from Brazil and Guyana.
origin: Europe, Asia, Africa, the Americas, Austria

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)
care.used_for_details: (NC State ethnobotany) Tannins extracted from rhizomes are used in dyeing wool a purple-black or brown
care.edible_parts: In some parts of the world the leaves and flowers are eaten in moderation.

description: Nymphaea tetragona is an aquatic perennial, species of flowering plant commonly called pygmy waterlily and small white water lily, belonging to the family Nymphaeaceae.
Nymphaea violacea, also known as blue lily, is a species of flowering plant in the family Nymphaeaceae.
origin: Europe, Asia, Africa, the Americas, Austria

care.used_for_details: (NC State ethnobotany) Tannins extracted from rhizomes are used in dyeing wool a purple-black or brown
